Client.title: Client
Client.description: Represents a client who uses the StylePro AI platform for booking appointments and AI hairstyle visualization.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Client entity.

email.description: The client's email address, used for communication and login (if applicable via external auth).

phone.description: The client's phone number, used for appointment reminders and contact.

notes.description: Internal notes about the client for CRM purposes, visible only to barbershop staff.

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the client record was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the client record was last updated.

Barber.title: Barber
Barber.description: Represents a barber working at the barbershop, including their profile, services, and schedule.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Barber entity.

email.description: The barber's email address for internal communication.

phone.description: The barber's phone number for internal communication.

bio.description: A short biography of the barber, highlighting their experience and specialties, for client viewing.

photoUrl.description: URL to the barber's profile photo.

serviceIds.description: References to Services this barber offers. (Relationship: Barber N:N Service)

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the barber record was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the barber record was last updated.

Service.title: Service
Service.description: Defines a service offered by the barbershop, including its details, pricing, and duration.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Service entity.

name.description: The name of the service (e.g., 'Haircut', 'Beard Trim').

description.description: A detailed description of the service.

durationMinutes.description: The estimated duration of the service in minutes.

isActive.description: Indicates if the service is currently available for booking.

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the service record was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the service record was last updated.

Appointment.title: Appointment
Appointment.description: Records a specific booking made by a client with a barber for a particular service.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Appointment entity.

clientId.description: Reference to the Client who booked the appointment. (Relationship: Client 1:N Appointment)

barberId.description: Reference to the Barber assigned to this appointment. (Relationship: Barber 1:N Appointment)

serviceId.description: Reference to the Service booked for this appointment. (Relationship: Service 1:N Appointment)

startTime.description: The scheduled start date and time of the appointment.

endTime.description: The scheduled end date and time of the appointment.

status.description: The current status of the appointment (e.g., 'Booked', 'Confirmed', 'Completed', 'Cancelled', 'No-Show').

clientNotes.description: Notes from the client regarding their preferences or requests for the appointment.

barberNotes.description: Internal notes from the barber or owner about the appointment.

confirmationSent.description: Indicates if a confirmation notification has been sent to the client.

reminderSent.description: Indicates if a reminder notification has been sent to the client.

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the appointment record was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the appointment record was last updated.

BarberScheduleBlock.title: BarberScheduleBlock
BarberScheduleBlock.description: Defines a recurring block of availability for a specific barber on a given day of the week.

id.description: Unique identifier for the BarberScheduleBlock entity.

barberId.description: Reference to the Barber whose schedule this block defines. (Relationship: Barber 1:N BarberScheduleBlock)

dayOfWeek.description: The day of the week for this schedule block (e.g., 'Monday', 'Tuesday', etc.).

startTime.description: The start time of the availability block (e.g., '09:00').

endTime.description: The end time of the availability block (e.g., '17:00').

isActive.description: Indicates if this schedule block is currently active and should be used for generating available slots.

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the schedule block record was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the schedule block record was last updated.

HairstyleVisualization.title: HairstyleVisualization
HairstyleVisualization.description: Stores a record of a client's AI hairstyle visualization session, including the original and generated images.

id.description: Unique identifier for the HairstyleVisualization entity.

clientId.description: Reference to the Client who performed the visualization. (Relationship: Client 1:N HairstyleVisualization)

originalImageUrl.description: URL to the client's original uploaded photo.

generatedImageUrls.description: An array of URLs to the AI-generated hairstyle images.

suggestedStyles.description: An array of names or identifiers for the suggested hairstyles in the generated images.

selectedStyle.description: The name or identifier of the hairstyle chosen by the client from the generated options (if any).

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the visualization record was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the visualization record was last updated.

definition.description: Stores individual client profiles. The 'userId' matches the authenticated user's UID, ensuring path-based ownership. Owners/staff can manage all client profiles. Clients can read/write their own profile.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the client, typically matching Firebase Authentication UID.

definition.description: Stores profiles for barbers. Clients can read public details. Barbers can manage their own profiles (where 'barberId' matches their UID). Owners can manage all barber profiles. No denormalized authorization fields are required as access is direct or role-based.

params.name: barberId
params.description: The unique identifier of the barber, typically matching Firebase Authentication UID.

definition.description: Defines services offered by the barbershop. This is a public collection, allowing all clients to browse available services. Only owners can create, update, or delete services.

params.name: serviceId
params.description: The unique identifier for the service.

definition.description: Stores all appointments. Includes denormalized 'clientId' and 'barberId' fields for authorization independence. Clients can manage their own appointments (via 'clientId'), barbers can manage theirs (via 'barberId'), and owners can manage all. Security rules will enforce query constraints for clients/barbers listing their own appointments.

params.name: appointmentId
params.description: The unique identifier for the appointment.

definition.description: Stores recurring availability blocks for barbers. Includes denormalized 'barberId' for authorization independence. Active blocks are publicly readable. Barbers can manage their own blocks, and owners can manage all. Security rules will enforce query constraints for barbers listing their own schedule blocks.

params.name: scheduleBlockId
params.description: The unique identifier for the barber's schedule block.

definition.description: Stores AI hairstyle visualization results for each client. This is private data, owned by the 'userId' in the path. Clients can manage their own visualizations. Owners can access all visualizations via a collection group query ('hairstyleVisualizations').

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the client who owns these visualizations, typically matching Firebase Authentication UID.

params.name: visualizationId
params.description: The unique identifier for a specific hairstyle visualization session.

definition.description: Dedicated collection for barbershop owner roles. The existence of a document at this path where 'ownerId' matches 'request.auth.uid' grants administrative privileges. This follows the DBAC 'Existence over Content' principle.

params.name: ownerId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user (client) who has the owner role, matching Firebase Authentication UID.

firestore.reasoning:
The recommended Firestore structure for StylePro AI prioritizes Authorization Independence, Structural Segregation, and adherence to Query-Aware Permissions (QAPs). 

**Authorization Independence:**
1.  **Path-based ownership:** For client-specific, private data like `Client` profiles (`/users/{userId}`) and `HairstyleVisualization` records (`/users/{userId}/hairstyleVisualizations/{visualizationId}`), ownership is directly inferred from the `userId` wildcard matching `request.auth.uid`. This eliminates the need for `get()` calls in security rules, allowing atomic operations and simplified authorization logic. 
2.  **Denormalized Authorization Fields:** For collaborative or cross-entity data like `Appointment` and `BarberScheduleBlock`, critical authorization fields (`clientId`, `barberId`) are explicitly denormalized within each document. This means rules can check `resource.data.clientId == request.auth.uid` or `resource.data.barberId == request.auth.uid` without ever needing to `get()` a parent or related document. This is crucial for maintaining authorization independence and enabling atomic batch writes and transactions.

**Query-Aware Permissions (QAPs):**
1.  **Structural Segregation for Public/Private:** Publicly accessible data (e.g., `Service` details, public `Barber` profiles, active `BarberScheduleBlock`s) is placed in top-level collections or documents with explicit `isActive` flags. This allows broad `list` access for public/client users while enabling strict write controls for owners/barbers. 
2.  **Enforcing Query Filters:** For collections like `Appointment` and `BarberScheduleBlock`, where clients/barbers need to view *only their own* data, security rules are designed to *enforce* specific `where` clauses (e.g., `request.query.where.clientId == request.auth.uid` or `request.query.where.barberId == request.auth.uid`). If a client or barber attempts to `list` without these required filters, the request will be denied, preventing data leakage and ensuring QAPs. Owners, by contrast, have broader `list` permissions. 
3.  **Collection Group for Nested Data (Owner Access):** For `HairstyleVisualization` data nested under `users/{userId}`, owners can use `collectionGroup('hairstyleVisualizations')` to query across all clients' visualizations. Security rules on this collection group ensure that only owners can perform such wide-ranging queries, while individual clients can only access their specific subcollection.

**DBAC and Invariants:**
*   Roles (`roles_owner`) are managed through document existence in dedicated collections, adhering to the DBAC principle. 
*   Timestamp fields (`createdAt`, `updatedAt`) are part of every entity's schema and can be enforced by rules as invariants.
*   The use of descriptive wildcards (e.g., `{userId}`, `{appointmentId}`) and consistent field naming (e.g., `clientId`, `barberId`) enhances clarity and debuggability. The `status` field in `Appointment` exemplifies explicit state modeling.
